channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia shares fall after it says US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chip makers slumped in after-hours trading and in Asia after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia shares fall after it says US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: Shares in Nvidia slumped in after-hours trading late Tuesday after it said that tighter U.S. government controls on its exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI wants to be more like Apple and Google
item.description: Could it be any clearer that Sam Altman intends for OpenAI to be a sprawling consumer tech company, not just an AI lab? His public comments certainly suggest as much.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cohere’s Embed 4 model helps enterprises search dynamic documents, ‘messy’ data
item.description: Embedding models help transform complex data — text, images, audio, and video — into numerical representations that computers can understand. The embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the data, making them useful for tasks like search, recommendation systems, and natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hertz says hackers stole customer data in vendor breach
item.description: Hertz is notifying customers that hackers may have stolen personal information including credit card details and Social Security numbers during a data breach on one of its vendors.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Notion Mail lets you build the inbox you actually want
item.description: Most email apps have strong opinions about what your inbox should look like. Notion Mail is the opposite. The new offshoot from productivity app Notion is all about flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tariffs on Key Imports: Trump Administration Launches Investigations
item.description: The Trump administration has taken its next steps toward imposing more tariffs on key imports, launching investigations into imports of computer chips, chip making equipment and pharmaceuticals.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk and Jack Dorsey want to kill IP law. That would be a huge mistake
item.description: Few statements communicate a sentiment more directly than the four words Jack Dorsey, a cofounder of Twitter, posted over the weekend on the platform he no longer controls: “delete all IP law,” was his simple message.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Intel to sell 51% Altera stake to Silver Lake amid cost cuts
item.description: Intel Corp. has agreed to sell a 51% stake in its programmable chips unit Altera to Silver Lake Management, making good on plans to start spinning off non-core assets.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Cisco SVP talks agentic AI, quantum security and sustainable infrastructure
item.description: When responsibility for a company’s long-term growth and differentiation in the market is part of your job description, it can be an adventure. Nathan Jokel, senior vice president of corporate strategy and alliances at Cisco, has stepped up to the challenge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iPads to Get More Mac-like Attributes This Year, Report Says
item.description: Apple is reportedly planning to give its iPads more Mac-like attributes this year, with improved window management and multitasking capabilities expected at WWDC 2025.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is quantum internet? Why it could change the internet forever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says quantum internet ensures unhackable security and redefines online privacy and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s tariff flip-flop: Policy shifts disrupt tech procurement for CIOs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Sunday he will reveal new tariff rates on imported semiconductors this week, the latest in a series of rapid policy shifts that have thrown the technology sector into confusion and forced businesses to reevaluate procurement strategies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4-legged hydrogen-powered robot you can actually ride
item.description: Kawasaki's CORLEO is a hydrogen-powered, AI-driven rideable robot. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reports on this new way of thinking about off-road transportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeepSeek’s open source movement
item.description: DeepSeek may have originated in China, but it stopped being Chinese the minute it was released on Hugging Face with an accompanying paper detailing its development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
